summary: This release introduces comprehensive device configuration management features including customer, site, device, and sensor lifecycle operations. It also adds security enhancements for role-based access control, time-based invitation tokens, and on-call shift protection, along with DC09 CMS marketplace integration and report scheduling capabilities.

items.title: Device Activation and Deactivation
items.description: Enables users to activate or deactivate devices through the configuration menu, controlling whether sensors on the device are operational.

items.title: Device Creation in Sites
items.description: Allows users to add new devices to existing sites with automatic sensor discovery, enabling comprehensive device management at the site level.

items.title: Device Deletion from Sites
items.description: Provides the ability to delete devices from sites, removing all associated sensors and configurations.

items.title: Device Rediscovery
items.description: Enables rediscovery of previously deleted devices, allowing recovery of device configurations and sensors without manual reconfiguration.

items.title: Site Creation and Management
items.description: Supports creating multiple sites within a customer, providing organizational structure for device deployment.

items.title: Site Deletion
items.description: Allows deletion of sites from customers, removing all associated devices and sensors.

items.title: Site Activation and Deactivation
items.description: Enables activating or deactivating sites to control operational status of all devices and sensors within a site.

items.title: Customer Creation at Service Provider Level
items.description: Provides the ability to create new customers at the service provider level, supporting multi-tenant architecture and hierarchical organization.

items.title: Customer Deletion
items.description: Enables deletion of customers from the service provider level, removing all associated sites, devices, and sensors.

items.title: Customer Naming with Numeric Characters
items.description: Supports creating customer names containing numeric characters, providing flexible naming conventions.

items.title: Customer Activation and Deactivation
items.description: Enables activating or deactivating customers to control operational status across all associated sites and devices.

items.title: Sensor Activation and Deactivation
items.description: Provides granular control over individual sensors within devices, allowing activation or deactivation without affecting the entire device.

items.title: Sensor Deletion from Devices
items.description: Enables deletion of individual sensors from devices, including bulk deletion of all sensors within a device.

items.title: Sensor Addition to Devices
items.description: Allows adding new sensors to devices, including the ability to create the same sensor across multiple devices.

items.title: Cross-Device Sensor Configuration
items.description: Supports creating the same sensor across multiple devices, enabling consistent sensor configuration for similar device types.

items.title: Service Provider Activation and Deactivation
items.description: Enables activating or deactivating service providers, which controls operational status of all customers, sites, devices, and sensors under that provider.

items.title: Device and Sensor Visibility
items.description: Provides comprehensive view of all devices and sensors with their associated details, supporting monitoring and management activities.

items.title: Continuous Alarm Event Flow
items.description: Ensures alarm events flow continuously through Talos workflow and VAS without interruption, maintaining real-time monitoring capabilities.

items.title: Alarm Assignment Operator Workflow
items.description: Enables alarm assignment flow from Talos workflow to Map and Salvo pages, providing operators with real-time alarm visibility across multiple interfaces.

items.title: Device Group Creation
items.description: Enables creation of device groups to organize and manage devices logically, supporting bulk operations and simplified device administration.

items.title: Master Admin Role Assignment Restriction
items.description: Master Admin role cannot be assigned through user invite. System prevents selection of Master Admin role during invite creation.

items.title: Invited User Permission Restrictions
items.description: Invited users cannot modify user roles or create new invites unless explicitly granted those permissions. Options are hidden or disabled, and direct API access returns authorization errors.

items.title: Backend Privilege Escalation Prevention
items.description: System blocks privilege escalation attempts at the backend level. Modified requests attempting to escalate roles return 403 Forbidden with no database changes.

items.title: Time-Based Invitation Tokens
items.description: System generates unique access tokens for time-based invitations. Tokens are linked to invited users and are unique in the database.

items.title: Token Time Window Validation
items.description: Tokens allow access only during defined time range. Access denied before start time and after expiration. No grace period unless specified.

items.title: Automatic Access Revocation on Token Expiry
items.description: User sessions are automatically invalidated when tokens expire. Users cannot perform actions and must re-login. Expired tokens cannot be reused.

items.title: Token Time Validation Security
items.description: Token validation is based on server time. Changing client timezone does not extend access. System prevents creation when start time equals end time.

items.title: Overlapping Time-Based Invites Handling
items.description: System correctly handles multiple overlapping time-based invites for same user. No privilege extension beyond defined windows.

items.title: On-Call Shift Detection Before Role Edit
items.description: System detects active or upcoming shifts before allowing role edits. Warning prompt appears when editing roles assigned to on-call users.

items.title: Role Edit Warning Details
items.description: Warning prompt lists impacted users by name with shift status (Active/Upcoming). Explains impact on on-call coverage.

items.title: Role Edit Decision Options
items.description: Cancel is default action. Immediate application requires extra confirmation. Scheduled application defaults to end of last active shift. Options are mutually exclusive.

items.title: Backend Enforcement of Shift Validation
items.description: Backend enforces shift validation. Direct API calls or URL manipulation cannot bypass prompt flow. Returns 403/validation error.

items.title: Role Change Audit Logging
items.description: Audit logs capture admin, timestamp, impacted users, before/after states, effective time, and decision type (Immediate/Scheduled/Cancelled).

items.title: Role Edit Without On-Call Users
items.description: No warning prompt appears when editing roles assigned to users with no active or upcoming shifts. Changes apply immediately.

items.title: Dynamic Shift Status Re-validation
items.description: System re-validates shift status before applying changes. If shifts end during edit flow, restrictions are lifted automatically.

items.title: Mixed Shift Status Handling
items.description: Warning lists only affected users when role has users with different shift statuses (Active, Upcoming, No shift).

items.title: Overlapping Shift Effective Time Calculation
items.description: Default effective time for scheduled changes is end of LAST active shift. No gap miscalculation for overlapping or back-to-back shifts.

items.title: Concurrent Admin Role Edit Handling
items.description: System detects version conflicts when multiple admins edit same role. Proper concurrency handling prevents silent overwrites.

items.title: Scheduled Change Conflict Handling
items.description: System handles conflicts when user gets new shift during scheduled role change period. Follows defined business rules.

items.title: Complex Scenario Audit Accuracy
items.description: Audit logs accurately capture decision type, full impacted users list, timestamps, before/after states, and admin ID for complex scenarios.

items.title: Large User Base Role Edit Performance
items.description: Shift detection and warning display complete within acceptable time for roles assigned to 500+ users. No timeout or partial listing.

items.title: Alarm Management System Category in Marketplace
items.description: Add Alarm Management System category to Marketplace with visibility of all CMS options (Evalink Talos, Amwin, Lisa, Immix, Alice, Other DC09-compatible). Display current configuration state and integration type for each CMS entry.

items.title: Admin-Only CMS Configuration Access
items.description: Restrict CMS configuration options to admin users only. Non-admin users can view CMS entries but cannot access configuration options.

items.title: Evalink Talos CMS Configuration
items.description: Enable Evalink Talos CMS configuration with Company ID and API Token fields. Support secure credential storage with masked token display after saving.

items.title: DC09 Account ID Field on Site Forms
items.description: Add DC09 Account ID field to Site Creation and Edit forms. Field is required for sites forwarding alarms to DC09-based CMS. Support visibility and editability for admin users.

items.title: Immediate DC09 Account ID Change Effect
items.description: Ensure DC09 Account ID changes take effect immediately. Alarms are routed according to the updated configuration without delay.

items.title: Manage Report Schedules Summary Dashboard
items.description: Add read-only summary cards at the top of Manage Report Schedules page displaying Active Schedules count, Reports Sent This Month, and Failed Deliveries. Cards are informational only and non-interactive.

items.title: Create New Report Schedule
items.description: Enable users to create new report schedules via 'Create New Schedule' button. Support filling required fields and saving with success confirmation. Created schedules appear in the schedule table.

items.title: Schedule Table Structure
items.description: Display schedule table with columns: Schedule, Customers, Site, and Actions. Table shows all configured report schedules with their associated data.

items.title: Schedule Actions (Edit, Delete, Trigger)
items.description: Provide Actions column with Edit, Delete, and Trigger functionality. Edit allows modifying schedule data. Delete removes schedule after confirmation. Trigger manually sends the report on demand.

items.title: Rows Per Page Pagination
items.description: Add 'Rows per page' option to schedule table allowing users to change the number of rows displayed. Table updates dynamically based on selection.

items.title: Auto Streaming Settings Management
items.description: Admin users can view and modify Auto Streaming settings directly in GCX. All settings are read from and written to Genesis with no local GCX storage. Settings include enabling/disabling auto streaming, selecting which alarm types trigger streaming, and managing streaming permissions by role.

items.title: Alarm-Triggered Auto Streaming
items.description: When a qualifying alarm is triggered, live streams automatically start for all cameras mapped to that alarm. Multiple cameras can stream simultaneously. Streams stop automatically when alarms are closed or acknowledged according to Genesis rules.

items.title: Zone-Based Camera Selection
items.description: Auto streaming respects zone definitions from Genesis. When an alarm is mapped to a specific zone, only cameras within that zone stream automatically. If no zones are defined, all site cameras stream by default. Zone changes affect future alarms only.

items.title: Streaming Permission Controls
items.description: Admins can set which roles are allowed to view streams. Operators without streaming permission cannot see or access streams, even via deep links. Access denial attempts are logged in audit logs.

items.title: Comprehensive Activity Logging
items.description: All streaming activity is logged including alarm ID, site, cameras streamed, user, and timestamps. Permission denials and streaming failures are also logged with full details for troubleshooting and compliance.

items.title: Site and Device Drag and Drop
items.description: Users can drag and drop sites or devices into the Salvo video viewer. When a second device is dropped over an existing one, the first stream closes and the second begins playing. Sites stream in default 2x2 view format.
